description: State retention manifest. Each entry declares a state path, owner, purpose, retention intent, and disposition. The disk report flags state paths >=100MB without an entry.

entries.purpose: Parent container for machine-local Codex state
entries.retention_intent: retain container; child paths carry the concrete retention decisions

entries.purpose: Parent container for project-specific state that is not source code
entries.retention_intent: retain container; child paths carry the concrete retention decisions

entries.purpose: Large local research sidecar data and JIT defect prediction state
entries.retention_intent: retain while BigData/Spark research work remains active; review size before archiving or pruning

entries.purpose: LaunchAgent repair state and local repair evidence
entries.retention_intent: review after the launchagent repair window closes; archive only if follow-up evidence is needed

entries.purpose: Local relay sessions and logs that support the registered infrastructure project
entries.retention_intent: retain while relay work remains active; rotate logs in the project surface when they grow

entries.purpose: Legacy local relay sessions retained as migration/reference state
entries.retention_intent: archive or prune after confirming the dual-relay surface has the needed continuity data

entries.purpose: Local review staging and candidate inventories
entries.retention_intent: retain while current review workflows are active; archive stale candidate bundles

entries.purpose: Temporary local staging for controlled workspace operations
entries.retention_intent: review monthly; promote durable evidence to ops docs or archive stale staging bundles
